steps.title: Count the data values
steps.content: The dataset has 9 values: 73, 75, 78, 80, 82, 86, 93, 97, 101. They are already arranged in ascending order.

steps.title: Find the median using the middle value rule
steps.content:
When a dataset has an odd number of values, the median is the middle value. With 9 values, the position of the median is:
$$\text{Position} = \frac{9 + 1}{2} = 5$$
Counting to the 5th position: 73 (1st), 75 (2nd), 78 (3rd), 80 (4th), 82 (5th). The median is 82. That confirms B is correct.
